commit.message:
feat(tools): multi-source stub-to-JSON converter for typeshed integration (#572)

## Summary
- Python converter that parses type stubs (`.pyi`) and PEP 561 annotated source (`.py`) files into the `StdlibModule` JSON format consumed by the Go sast-engine
- Supports three source types: typeshed (195 packages), external stub repos (django-stubs), and PEP 561 packages (Flask, SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, etc.)
- Extracts functions, classes (with methods + attributes + properties), constants, module attributes
- Handles `@overload`, `Optional`/`Union` unwrapping, PEP 604 `|`, `@property` → attributes, `__init__` return types
- Generates per-package JSON + `manifest.json` with SHA256 checksums
- Submodule merging via `__init__.pyi` re-export detection

## Validation
- **193 typeshed packages** converted with zero errors
- **9,009 functions**, **10,933 classes**, **36,491 attributes** extracted
- 101 tests passing, 94% code coverage
- All 194 output JSON files valid and parseable

## Files
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `sast-engine/tools/typeshed-converter/convert.py` | Core converter (~470 LoC) |
| `sast-engine/tools/typeshed-converter/test_convert.py` | 101 tests |
| `sast-engine/tools/typeshed-converter/sources.yaml` | Multi-source config |
| `sast-engine/tools/typeshed-converter/requirements.txt` | Python deps |

## Test plan
- [x] Parses typeshed stubs and produces valid JSON
- [x] Handles `@overload` — picks first non-None return type
- [x] Handles `Optional[X]` → X, `Union[X, None]` → X, `X | None` → X
- [x] Extracts class attributes (`ast.AnnAssign`) and `@property` methods
- [x] `__init__` return types set to class FQN
- [x] Submodule merging: `flask.app.Flask` → `flask.Flask` via `__init__.pyi`
- [x] Manifest includes SHA256 checksums for all modules
- [x] `python -m pytest test_convert.py -v --cov` passes with 94% coverage

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

elements to be parsed into the AST and mapped to React components.

commit.message:
feat: lazy-load AI providers to slim dependency tree (#10)

Convert all 17+ AI provider SDKs from static imports to dynamic imports
via lazy factory functions. Move provider SDKs, AWS/Google auth, and
platform-specific deps to optionalDependencies. Remove 6 unused deps
(clipboardy, chokidar, partial-json, @openauthjs/openauth, @pierre/diffs,
opentui-spinner).

This makes `npm install @aictrl/cli` viable — the required dependency
tree is now much smaller since provider SDKs are only loaded when
actually used.

Co-authored-by: Bulat Skill7 <bulat@aictrl.dev>

commit.message:
compile: execution-aware cell status and Child Workflows refinements

Blank cells now carry meaningful status instead of empty strings:
- [EXECUTABLE]: workflow not in execution (no fields filled anywhere)
- [READY]: node's prerequisites met, ready to execute
- [BLOCKED]: node's prerequisites not yet satisfied
- N/A: node was executed but field left blank, or auto-managed with no spawn

Child Workflows column: N/A only appears when the node had subprocess potential
(spawns_workflow edges) but nothing was spawned. Nodes with no subprocess
edges show nothing in that column. Section renderer suppresses Child Workflows
line when irrelevant. Reverse-edge graph computed once per compile call.

* docs(AMA-525): wearable form feedback spike design

Platform architecture (Apple Watch/Wear OS/Garmin adapter pattern),
ML strategy (Approach A classical + B Core ML/LiteRT, C north star),
haptic language vocabulary v0.1, partner device ecosystem (GymAware,
Output Sports), and Apple Watch PoC scope for squat depth detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ama-1066): Sentry -> Linear auto-sync via scheduled GitHub Action

Polls Sentry every 4h for new/regressed issues and creates Linear
tickets automatically. Filter rules:
- Fatal (crashes): always create
- Regressions: always create
- App Hangs/ANRs: only if >=3 users affected
- New errors: only if >=2 users affected

State is persisted via GitHub Actions cache to prevent duplicates.
Linear ticket links are written back to Sentry as issue notes.

No human intervention required.

Resolves AMA-1066

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add pr-comments skill (#28)

## Summary
- Adds a new `pr-review` skill that fetches open PR review threads,
decides which comments to implement vs. decline, and closes the loop
- Uses `gh pr view` / REST API for most operations; only uses GraphQL
for thread resolution (the one thing REST can't do)
- Gives credit to reviewers via `Co-authored-by` trailers in the commit
message

## How it works
1. Fetches inline review comments via `gh api
repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/comments`
2. Uses a focused GraphQL query only to get thread node IDs + resolution
state
3. Reads current file state at each referenced location to judge
validity
4. Presents a plan for user confirmation before making any changes
5. Implements valid changes, commits with commenter credit, replies to
declined threads, resolves implemented threads via GraphQL mutation

## Test Plan
- [ ] Invoke skill on a PR with a mix of valid and invalid Copilot
review comments
- [ ] Verify valid comments are implemented and threads resolved
- [ ] Verify invalid comments receive a reply and threads remain open
- [ ] Verify `Co-authored-by` trailers appear in the commit

---
Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
feat: Phase 6.4c shared folder hardening — permissions, cleanup, tests (#199)

Reactive 403 handling: on HTTP 403, query ListItemPermissions API to
authoritatively determine read-only vs transient. Walk up folder
hierarchy to find permission boundary. Record local_issue at boundary
folder. Planner suppresses writes under denied folders with on-demand
subfolder override checks. Periodic recheck clears issues when
permissions are granted.

FC-12 (B-309): deleteLocalFolder removes disposable files (.DS_Store,
Thumbs.db, ._*, .tmp, desktop.ini, etc.) before attempting rmdir.

B-332: reconcileShortcutScopes test coverage (delta, enumerate,
collision, error isolation).

B-333/334/335: Thread shortcut list through functions (4→1 DB queries),
pre-filter delete IDs to known shortcuts, collapse duplicate observation
case.

B-317: API response fuzz tests (DriveItem, DeltaResponse, Permission).
B-318: Fault injection tests (context cancel, baseline error, partial
file cleanup).
B-319: Clock skew resilience audit doc + targeted tests.
B-324: ci_issues.md §19 mitigation status update.

Additional: nested shortcut detection (skip with warning), duplicate
source folder dedup in collision detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(board): add tmux pane utilities for zen mode orchestration

New module src/board/tmux-pane.ts with functions for tmux pane management:
- agentWindowName: get tmux window name for agent sessions
- windowExists: check if a named tmux window exists
- joinAgentPane: pull agent pane into current window as right split
- breakPane: send pane back to its own window (preserves session)
- splitWithInfo: create info pane for issues without agents
- killPane/isPaneAlive: cleanup and health checks

All functions handle tmux errors gracefully (return null/false).
Unit tests mock execFileSync for all tmux commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(board): wire zen mode to tmux pane orchestration

- Enter zen: detect agent window, join-pane or create info split
- Cursor-follow: swap right pane when navigating between issues
- Exit zen: break agent pane back to its window or kill info pane
- Launch from zen: C key swaps right pane to show new agent session
- Dead pane detection: 2s interval auto-exits zen if pane killed externally

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Block digit keys 1/2 when left panel is hidden (focus repos/statuses no-op)

- Add tests for left-panel-hidden digit key guards

commit.message:
fix: add unique constraint to reconciliation_notes and simplify upsertShiftNote

Adds UNIQUE(entity_type, entity_id) to reconciliation_notes, replacing the
non-unique index. This enables upsertShiftNote to use a true atomic
INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE instead of the backup-restore pattern,
eliminating the transient window where a note could be absent mid-operation.
